name: python3-stix2-patterns
title: python3-stix2-patterns
summary: tool to check the syntax of the CTI STIX Pattern expressions (Python 3)
description:
tool to check the syntax of the CTI STIX Pattern expressions (Python 3)
This package contains software tool for checking the syntax of the Cyber
Threat Intelligence (CTI) STIX Pattern expressions, which are used within STIX
to express conditions (prepresented with the Cyber Observable data model) that
indicate particular cyber threat activity. The repository contains source
code, an ANTLR grammar, automated tests and associated documentation for the
tool. The validator can be used as a command-line tool or as a Python library
which can be included in other applications.
.
This package installs the library for Python 3.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for validate-patterns.
arguments.raw: -patterns  Option present in package usage line for validate-patterns.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use validate-patterns because tool to check the syntax of the CTI STIX Pattern expressions (Python 3).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use validate-patterns as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `validate-patterns [options] [args]` as documented upstream for validate-patterns.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair validate-patterns with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `validate-patterns --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -patterns
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for validate-patterns.
